D4 Colorimetry L2 Understand Atom

Tracing the Horseshoe

sweep one pure wavelength across the spectrum and watch its chromaticity draw out the famous horseshoe boundary

Every pure (monochromatic) wavelength plots to a single point on the xy diagram. March from violet to red and those points trace the spectral locus — the outer curve of all visible colour. The straight line back from red to violet is the line of purples, which no single wavelength can make.

Slide the wavelength. The locus is drawn up to the current λ; its point and spectral colour are marked. The horseshoe is simply the path the standard observer's matching functions carve through chromaticity space.
